executiveSummary:
**Executive Summary: Medicaid Program Performance Analysis**

National Medicaid enrollment totaled 74.9 million beneficiaries as of February 2026, with all major states experiencing enrollment declines following the conclusion of pandemic-era continuous coverage protections. Year-over-year decreases ranged from minimal in North Carolina (0.0%) to significant in Michigan (-7.6%), suggesting varied state approaches to eligibility redeterminations. California leads with 12.4 million enrollees despite a 6.8% decline, while Texas maintains 4.0 million enrollees with a moderate 3.8% reduction. Total program expenditures reached $215.9 billion in fiscal year 2024, with a national average per-enrollee cost of $2,883. Spending efficiency varies considerably across states, with Florida achieving the lowest per-enrollee cost at $2,036 compared to Pennsylvania's $4,363, while Texas operates at $3,014 per enrollee—slightly above the national average but competitive among large states.

Quality performance remains a critical concern, with the national average Medicaid quality score at 65.6 out of 100 points in 2024. Only 10 states achieved high-performer status (≥75 points), led by Massachusetts (81) and Minnesota (80), while 15 states scored below 60 points. Texas ranked 30th nationally with a score of 62, placing it in the moderate performance tier but trailing peer states like North Carolina and Illinois. Managed care penetration has reached 83.2% nationally, with Texas exceeding this benchmark at 93.3% penetration covering 3.9 million enrollees. Recent regulatory developments signal increased federal oversight, including new community engagement requirements and provider payment restrictions, while procurement activities in Texas and other major states indicate ongoing managed care contract renewals that may impact program delivery and costs in the coming fiscal year.
texasSpotlight:
## Texas Medicaid Program Performance Analysis

Texas operates the nation's third-largest Medicaid program with nearly 4 million enrollees, yet exhibits a mixed performance profile that reflects both operational strengths and systemic challenges. The state demonstrates strong managed care implementation with 93.3% penetration—significantly above the national average of 83.2%—suggesting effective care coordination infrastructure. Texas also maintains relatively controlled per-enrollee spending at $3,014, which sits between the efficient southern peer states like Florida ($2,036) and North Carolina ($2,623) and more expensive programs in expansion states. The state's enrollment decline of 3.8% year-over-year aligns closely with national unwinding trends and performs better than large-state peers like California (-6.8%) and Michigan (-7.6%), indicating measured post-pandemic normalization.

However, Texas faces persistent quality challenges that demand attention. With a quality score of 62/100, ranking 30th nationally, the state significantly underperforms compared to high-achieving large states and falls into the "moderate" tier—concerning given its substantial resources and managed care penetration. While Texas avoids the bottom-tier performance of southern peers like Arkansas (54) and Alabama (52), its quality metrics lag behind what would be expected for a state with nearly $12 billion in annual Medicaid expenditures. The combination of high managed care adoption and mediocre quality outcomes suggests opportunities for enhanced performance-based contracting and care quality initiatives. As Texas continues to navigate federal regulatory changes around community engagement requirements and state-directed payments, the state's ability to leverage its managed care infrastructure to drive quality improvements will be critical to watch, particularly given its status as the largest non-expansion state serving a complex, diverse population.

alerts.title: Pennsylvania Per-Enrollee Spending 51% Above National Average
alerts.description: Pennsylvania's per-enrollee spending of $4,363 is dramatically higher than the national average of $2,883, indicating potential cost control issues or unique population needs.

alerts.title: Florida Medicaid Spending Unusually Low at $2,036 Per Enrollee
alerts.description: Florida's per-enrollee spending is 29% below the national average despite having 3.6M enrollees, suggesting potential access or adequacy concerns.

alerts.title: Five States Show Critically Low Quality Scores Below 55
alerts.description: Arkansas, South Carolina, Alabama, Oklahoma, and Mississippi all scored below 55/100 on quality measures, significantly below the national average of 65.6.

alerts.title: Massachusetts High Spending with Excellent Quality Outcomes
alerts.description: Massachusetts combines the highest quality score (81/100) with high per-enrollee spending ($4,221), demonstrating a successful high-investment model.

alerts.title: CMS Announces Nationwide Provider Enrollment Moratorium
alerts.description: Federal Register shows CMS has implemented temporary nationwide moratoria on Medicare and Medicaid provider enrollment, potentially affecting network adequacy.

executiveInsights.title: Texas Quality Score at Risk (62/100) - Below National Average
executiveInsights.summary: Texas ranks #30 of 50 states with a quality score of 62/100, falling below the national average of 65.6. This places Texas in the bottom 40% nationally and in the 'moderate' performance tier.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Poor quality performance could trigger CMS oversight, impact federal funding, or create contract risk for Accenture's Medicaid management services. Quality improvement initiatives may need immediate prioritization.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Flag quality improvement strategy to HHSC leadership immediately

executiveInsights.title: Texas Medicaid Enrollment Declining 3.8% YoY - 4M Population at Risk
executiveInsights.summary: Texas Medicaid enrollment dropped 3.8% year-over-year to 3,997,752 enrollees, representing a significant population decrease. This decline is consistent with national trends but indicates substantial coverage loss.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Enrollment decline directly impacts contract revenue and scope of services. Accenture needs to adjust operational capacity and help HHSC understand drivers to maintain program sustainability.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Review enrollment projections and contract volume assumptions with HHSC

executiveInsights.title: Texas Per-Enrollee Costs Above National Average ($3,014 vs $2,883)
executiveInsights.summary: Texas spends $3,014 per Medicaid enrollee, which is $131 above the national average of $2,883. Total Texas Medicaid expenditures reached $12.0 billion in fiscal year 2024.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Higher per-enrollee costs suggest potential inefficiencies or cost management opportunities that Accenture could address. This cost differential represents millions in potential savings across the 4M enrollee population.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Flag cost efficiency analysis opportunity to HHSC finance team

executiveInsights.title: CMS Provider Enrollment Moratorium Signals Fraud Crackdown
executiveInsights.summary: Recent Federal Register notices show CMS implementing nationwide temporary moratoria on provider enrollment in Medicare and Medicaid programs. Multiple notices appeared in May 2026 indicating heightened oversight.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Provider enrollment freezes typically indicate fraud concerns and will require Accenture to adjust provider network management and implement enhanced screening processes for Texas Medicaid.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Review provider enrollment procedures with HHSC compliance team

executiveInsights.title: Texas Managed Care Penetration at 93.3% - Above National Benchmark
executiveInsights.summary: Texas achieves 93.3% managed care penetration (3,863,808 enrollees), significantly above the national average of 83.2%. This represents strong managed care adoption in the state.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: High managed care penetration indicates Texas's operational maturity and validates Accenture's managed care expertise as valuable. This positions the state well for value-based care initiatives.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Leverage Texas MC success story in other state proposals

riskOpportunity.label: Enrollment decline trend
riskOpportunity.description: Texas Medicaid enrollment dropping 3.8% YoY could signal unwinding completion or eligibility tightening affecting program scope.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Develop analytics capabilities to predict enrollment stabilization and right-size operations accordingly.

riskOpportunity.label: Quality score gap
riskOpportunity.description: Texas ranks 30th nationally at 62/100 quality score, creating vulnerability in value-based contracts and federal oversight.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Propose comprehensive quality improvement initiative targeting specific measures where Texas underperforms.

riskOpportunity.label: Managed care recompete
riskOpportunity.description: High 93.3% managed care penetration suggests potential MCO contract renewals or recompetes affecting systems integration needs.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Monitor HHSC procurement portal for MCO RFPs and position for systems modernization opportunities.

riskOpportunity.label: Federal regulatory shifts
riskOpportunity.description: Multiple CMS regulatory changes on community engagement and state directed payments could require significant system modifications.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Establish dedicated regulatory tracking team to assess compliance gaps and propose proactive solutions.

riskOpportunity.label: OIG oversight exposure
riskOpportunity.description: Active HHS OIG Medicaid oversight with multiple workplan items creates audit risk but also compliance consulting opportunities.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Conduct proactive compliance assessment and develop OIG-ready documentation standards for client protection.

riskOpportunity.label: Provider enrollment moratorium
riskOpportunity.description: Nationwide temporary moratorium on provider enrollment could disrupt network adequacy and create system workflow changes.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Develop contingency plans for provider onboarding process modifications and network monitoring capabilities.

riskOpportunity.label: Cost efficiency pressure
riskOpportunity.description: Texas per-enrollee costs at $3,014 versus national average $2,883 creates pressure for cost containment initiatives.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Propose data analytics solution to identify cost reduction opportunities while maintaining quality outcomes.

riskOpportunity.label: Competitive positioning risk
riskOpportunity.description: Multiple states showing procurement activity suggests increased competition for large-scale Medicaid transformation contracts.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Strengthen Texas-specific differentiators and develop rapid response capability for emerging procurement opportunities.
